friction_items.description: src/research_os_humanities/protocols/textual/close_reading.yaml line 94 still references `tool_humanities_apparatus_audit` in step prose ('If the audit `tool_humanities_apparatus_audit` is available (planned; see docs/ROADMAP.md)...'). The tool is still not in TOOL_DEFINITIONS, not in _HANDLERS, not in _ALIASES. Preflight's `Protocol tool refs all resolve` check passes (24/24) because the phantom is only in description prose, not in a hard `tool:` callout — but an AI reading the protocol via sys_protocol_get format=full WILL still see it and try to call it. The new format=summary default does mask this on the first read (apparatus_audit ref count 0 in summary, 1 in full), but the gap is unfixed in the source. This is the single un-shipped baseline IOU.

src/research_os_humanities/router_entries.py still uses `sub_intent: method_pick` for close_reading, distant_reading, hermeneutic_method, digital_humanities_workflow, and scholarly_edition. Core hierarchy uses `pick`. tool_route still surfaces this drift in its response (sub_intent: 'method_pick'). No preflight check catches the pack↔core sub_intent mismatch.

src/research_os_humanities/__init__.py still pins `__version__ = '1.9.3'` while core is `__version__ = '2.0.0-dev'`. Every humanities pack protocol still declares `version: '1.9.3'` in its YAML. Preflight reports humanities@1.9.3 alongside qualitative@1.11.0 — the drift is now visible in preflight output but is not enforced as a check. Phase 16 release-prep must bump these in lockstep with 2.0.0.

close_reading.yaml line 235 still has `next_protocol: humanities/citation/citation_chains` (and `next_protocol_kind: forward_default`). humanities_essay_structure's `trigger:` block still claims it fires from close_reading.next_protocol — same self-inconsistency as baseline. The chain close_reading → humanities_essay_structure (which is the close-reading→essay scenario the validator runs) is still indirect, requires user override. Baseline LOW issue: this is unfixed.

close_reading.yaml description is still 50+ lines of literary-theory exposition (I.A. Richards, New Critics, Empson, de Man, Brooks, Wimsatt, Beardsley). Token_estimate still 1500. The new format=summary default cuts the BURDEN by ~70% (summary 5KB vs full 17KB) which substantially reduces the impact of this issue, but the underlying source is unchanged. The new default mitigates the symptom without addressing the root cause.

humanities/textual/close_reading router entry still has no `shortcut_tool` field. tool_route correctly returns `shortcut_tool: null`. distant_reading and digital_humanities_workflow also still missing. Baseline MEDIUM, demoted to LOW because tool_route's new `recommended_action` field (e.g. `sys_protocol_get(protocol_name='humanities/textual/close_reading', format='summary')`) gives a clean one-call entry even without a shortcut_tool. Recommended_action partially substitutes.

convoluted_tools: sys_protocol_* cluster still 6 tools (sys_protocol_get, _list, _next, _validate, _log, _history). Baseline called for collapse to 1-2 via operation= kwarg; consolidation did not reach this cluster.
sys_checkpoint_* cluster still 3 tools (sys_checkpoint_create, _rollback, _list). Could be one with operation=create|rollback|list.
confusing_protocols: humanities/textual/close_reading — description body still dominated by literary-theory exposition; default summary load mitigates this but source is unchanged.
humanities/method/hermeneutic_method — sub_intent 'method_pick' is itself a method, not a method-picker (taxonomy collision unchanged from baseline).
never_called_tools: tool_humanities_apparatus_audit (still phantom-referenced in close_reading.yaml description; no handler)
sys_protocol_validate (no protocol guidance points to it; tested via preflight not at MCP surface)
tool_dashboard (still loaded as a single tool in humanities scope is correct; but never called in close_reading flow — pack-aware filtering correctly excludes it from active_tools=13)
improvements_vs_baseline: TOOL COUNT 204 → 146 (-28%). The 5-variant figure-audit cluster collapsed to a single tool_audit with scope+dimension args. tool_dashboard collapsed from 7 dashboard_* tools to one. tool_step, tool_lessons, tool_reliability all consolidated.

NEW METADATA: every tool now carries `status: live|alias|deprecated` (146/146 = live) and `pack: core|humanities|...` (123 core + 23 pack-scoped). The maintainer can now scan one column to see what is core vs domain and what is shippable vs deprecated.

PACK-AWARE FILTERING: sys_active_tools(protocol='humanities/textual/close_reading') returns 13 active tools — a humanities researcher no longer wades through 50+ STEM-only tools (slurm_*, r_exec, notebook_*, sensitivity_*, dashboard_*). Cognitive surface for a close-reading session drops by ~90%.

MCP INSTRUCTIONS at handshake: server.py boot announces the canonical sequence (sys_boot → tool_route → sys_protocol_get(summary) → sys_active_tools). An AI client no longer has to discover the ritual by calling sys_help. This is a substantial usability win.

DEFAULT FORMAT=summary on sys_protocol_get: close_reading default load 5004 chars (was 17023 with format=full). 70% reduction in token cost on the routine protocol-load path. Crucially: the phantom apparatus_audit reference is HIDDEN by the new default (count=0 in summary, count=1 in full), which masks the un-shipped IOU on the most-common access path.

tool_route now returns `recommended_action` field with a concrete next call (e.g. `sys_protocol_get(protocol_name='humanities/textual/close_reading', format='summary')`). Removes one round of AI guesswork; partially substitutes for missing shortcut_tool fields.
tool_route also returns `why_matched` field with semantic similarity score and confidence — a maintainer can now see WHY the router picked a protocol vs grepping logs.

SERVER.PY REFACTORED into modular src/research_os/server/ package (entry.py, handlers/, dispatch.py, registry.py, aliases.py, tool_definitions/, optional_deps.py, rate_limiter.py). Was a single 7499-line file. Maintainer navigation is dramatically easier.
ALIAS TABLE: 80 aliases registered, preserves backward compatibility through the consolidation. No deprecated-alias tool refs in protocols (preflight check is clean across 78 deprecated names).

PREFLIGHT: now 24 checks (was 22 at baseline). New checks include 'Every tool definition has a handler' (146 tools wired) and 'No deprecated-alias tool refs in protocols'. Catches more wiring drift at CI time.
remaining_recommendations: Phase 16: SHIP tool_humanities_apparatus_audit OR remove the phantom reference from close_reading.yaml line 94. The new format=summary default masks the issue but does not fix it — an AI reading format=full still sees a phantom callout. Adding a preflight check that scans protocol DESCRIPTION prose (not just step tool: fields) for ``tool_<name>`` patterns and validates them against TOOL_DEFINITIONS would catch this.

Phase 16: BUMP humanities pack __version__ + every pack protocol's `version:` field from 1.9.3 to 2.0.0 in lockstep with the core 2.0.0 bump. Add a preflight check that pack protocols' version field matches the pack __version__ value.

Phase 16: RENAME humanities pack router_entries sub_intent `method_pick` → `pick` to match the core hierarchy in _router_index.yaml. Add a preflight check that every pack router_entry's (intent_class, sub_intent) tuple exists in the core hierarchy block.

Phase 16: FIX close_reading.next_protocol to chain to humanities_essay_structure for the close-reading→essay path (or add a conditional branch). Current chain points to citation_chains, but humanities_essay_structure's trigger claims it fires from close_reading.next_protocol — self-inconsistency.

v2.1.0 follow-up: COLLAPSE sys_protocol_* cluster (6 tools → 1-2 with operation= kwarg) and sys_checkpoint_* cluster (3 tools → 1). Phase 9 consolidation stopped before reaching these system-meta clusters.

v2.1.0 follow-up: TRIM close_reading.yaml description from 50 lines of literary-theory exposition to ~10 lines operational summary, move the lineage to an optional `background:` block. Even with format=summary masking most of it, the source bloat slows protocol authoring + review.

v2.1.0 follow-up: ADD tool_reliability operation=`unused_tools` (scan _HANDLERS vs reliability.jsonl in a project; emit list of never-called handlers since N days). The baseline asked for this; current tool_reliability has only operation=log_event|report. The maintainer telemetry blindspot from baseline is only partly closed.
